fields.narrative_intro: *An unqualified person and a gobsmacked person discuss LLM document corruption at an abandoned theme park.*
fields.panel1:
[setup]
UNQUALIFIED PERSON: "I let GPT handle my crystallography report."
GOBSMACKED PERSON: "The whole thing?"
fields.panel2:
[build]
UNQUALIFIED PERSON: "Nineteen frontier models tested. They're basically pros."
GOBSMACKED PERSON: "Sure, sure."
fields.panel3:
[pivot]
GOBSMACKED PERSON: "DELEGATE-52 says they corrupt 25% of content."
UNQUALIFIED PERSON: "Silently. Over time."
fields.panel4:
[silent reaction]
[Visual: Both characters stare at a printout. Every fourth line is replaced with gibberish. Behind them, an abandoned roller coaster car slowly rolls past, carrying only a stack of corrupted documents.]
fields.panel5:
[punch]
GOBSMACKED PERSON: "'Sparse but severe.' That's my thesis."
UNQUALIFIED PERSON: "Was."
fields.panel6:
[button]
[Visual: A defunct theme-park sign now reads 'DELEGATE-LAND: Where 25% of Your Document Goes to Die.' Unqualified person adds agentic tool use. Nothing improves.]

narrative_summary: A character who trusted an LLM with a full professional document discovers — via DELEGATE-52's damning 25% corruption finding — that their work was quietly, severely ruined, punchlined by the benchmark's own phrase 'sparse but severe' describing their now-destroyed thesis.
rationale: Candidate 3 earns its punchline — 'Sparse but severe. That's my thesis.' / 'Was.' is a clean two-beat closer that weaponizes the paper's own clinical language against the character without restating the setup, and the specificity (DELEGATE-52, 19 frontier models, 25% corruption, crystallography) is tightly woven rather than dropped in as a list. Candidate 4 ties on total but the 'chapter four just says music notation forty times' gag slightly oversells the absurdism, diluting the deadpan precision that makes the 'sparse but severe' callback land.
story_title: LLMs corrupt your documents when you delegate
hat_pair: full-face motorcycle helmet with googly eyes stuck on
